name: extend-signal-schema
description: Safely extend or refine AFI signal schemas and closely-related validators in afi-core, while preserving determinism, respecting PoI/PoInsight design, and obeying the AFI Droid Charter and AFI Core AGENTS.md boundaries.
capabilities: Extend existing AFI Protocol signal schemas with new fields and types
Generate TypeScript interfaces from schema definitions
Validate schema changes for backward compatibility
Create validation code for extended signal schemas
Document schema evolution and migration paths
Generate test cases for schema validation
author: AFI-Protocol
securitySummary: All 109 static findings are false positives from pattern matching against documentation and metadata files. The skill contains no executable code, only markdown documentation (SKILL.md) and JSON metadata (skill-report.json). All detected patterns are backtick-wrapped code examples in documentation or JSON string values describing the skill's functionality.
valueStatement: Schema evolution in distributed systems requires careful validation and backward compatibility. This skill helps you extend AFI Protocol signal schemas while maintaining type safety and generating proper validation code.
userTitle: Extend AFI Protocol Signal Schemas with Type Safety
limitations: Specific to AFI Protocol signal schema format and conventions
Requires existing AFI Protocol codebase structure
Does not handle runtime schema migration or data transformation

useCases.title: Add New Signal Fields
useCases.description: Extend existing signal schemas with new optional or required fields while maintaining backward compatibility with consumers

useCases.title: Design Schema Evolution Strategy
useCases.description: Plan and document schema versioning approach with proper deprecation paths for signal interface changes

useCases.title: Generate Validation Code
useCases.description: Automatically create TypeScript validation functions and type guards for extended signal schemas with test coverage

promptTemplates.title: Add Optional Field
promptTemplates.prompt: Add an optional timestamp field called lastModified to the UserSignal schema with proper TypeScript typing

promptTemplates.title: Extend with Nested Object
promptTemplates.prompt: Extend the TransactionSignal schema to include a nested metadata object with fields for source, category, and tags array

promptTemplates.title: Validate Breaking Changes
promptTemplates.prompt: Check if adding a required email field to the ContactSignal schema would break existing consumers and suggest migration path

promptTemplates.title: Generate Full Schema Package
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a new OrderStatusSignal schema with status enum, timestamp, orderId, and optional notes field then generate TypeScript interfaces, validators, and unit tests

bestPractices: Always add new fields as optional initially to maintain backward compatibility with existing signal consumers
Include comprehensive JSDoc comments describing field purpose, constraints, and examples in generated TypeScript interfaces
Generate unit tests for schema validators covering both valid cases and boundary conditions before deployment
antiPatterns: Adding required fields to existing schemas without coordinating consumer updates across all services
Changing field types or removing fields without proper deprecation period and migration tooling
Generating validation code without corresponding test coverage and integration validation

faq.question: What is an AFI Protocol signal schema?
faq.answer: A signal schema defines the structure and validation rules for data messages exchanged between AFI Protocol services using TypeScript types.

faq.question: Can this skill modify existing schema fields?
faq.answer: Yes, but it will warn about breaking changes and suggest backward-compatible alternatives like deprecation paths and field versioning.

faq.question: Does it work with other schema systems besides AFI Protocol?
faq.answer: No, this skill is specifically designed for AFI Protocol conventions and project structure. Use generic schema tools for other systems.

faq.question: What validation libraries does it support?
faq.answer: It generates TypeScript type guards and can integrate with common libraries like Zod, Yup, or io-ts based on project configuration.

faq.question: How does it handle schema versioning?
faq.answer: It follows AFI Protocol versioning conventions, suggesting version bumps and maintaining compatibility matrices for signal consumers.

faq.question: Can it generate schemas from existing code?
faq.answer: No, it extends existing schemas or creates new ones from specifications. Use TypeScript extraction tools for reverse engineering.
